features.summary: Apple reported Q1 Fiscal Year 2024 revenue of $119.6 billion, a 2% increase year-over-year, with EPS at $2.18, up 16%. The company achieved record revenues in multiple regions, including Europe and Asia-Pacific, and saw strong growth in services, which reached an all-time high of $23.1 billion. The installed base of active devices surpassed 2.2 billion, and the iPhone 15 lineup contributed significantly to revenue growth, with iPhone sales at $69.7 billion, up 6% year-over-year.
features.management_outlook: Looking ahead, Apple expects revenue for the March quarter to be similar to the previous year, factoring in a challenging comparison due to last year's channel fill and supply constraints. The company anticipates continued double-digit growth in services and a gross margin between 46% and 47%. Management remains optimistic about the long-term potential of emerging markets and the upcoming launch of Apple Vision Pro.
features.risks: Apple faces potential headwinds from foreign exchange fluctuations and macroeconomic conditions that could impact revenue. The company also noted the challenges of comparing results to the previous year, which included an extra week of sales and supply chain disruptions due to COVID-19. Additionally, competition in key markets like China remains a concern, with recent performance showing a decline in that region.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of growth include the strong performance of the iPhone 15 lineup, which has received positive customer feedback and set records for upgraders. The services segment continues to expand, with significant growth in paid subscriptions and customer engagement. Emerging markets, particularly India and Indonesia, are also contributing to revenue growth, alongside the anticipated impact of the new Apple Vision Pro device.
